chronology_steps.goal: Introduce new writer and reader shape without breaking the prior reader window.
chronology_steps.reader_window_expectation: reader window remains open to the prior protected bundle set
chronology_steps.rollback_posture: ROLLBACK_ALLOWED while the prior reader window still validates
chronology_steps.writer_posture: new writers may emit the expanded shape while older readers remain supported

chronology_steps.goal: Backfill or transform durable truth idempotently while preserving frozen historical references.
chronology_steps.reader_window_expectation: in-flight and sealed manifests continue on their frozen basis
chronology_steps.rollback_posture: ROLLBACK_ALLOWED only if backfill remains reversible or compatibility-preserving
chronology_steps.writer_posture: both expanded and legacy-readable state may coexist

chronology_steps.goal: Prove reader-window, replay, restore, migration, and client-window safety under the shared compatibility gate.
chronology_steps.reader_window_expectation: historical-manifest and replay or restore readability remain explicitly green
chronology_steps.rollback_posture: ROLLBACK_ALLOWED only if the compatibility gate says the rollback window is still open
chronology_steps.writer_posture: candidate build and schema bundle are judged together

chronology_steps.goal: Remove legacy shape only after no protected in-flight, replay, restore, or client window depends on it.
chronology_steps.reader_window_expectation: compatibility window closes only after protected historical and operational readers are safe
chronology_steps.rollback_posture: FAIL_FORWARD_ONLY once the compatibility window closes or destructive change begins
chronology_steps.writer_posture: old readers and fields are removed in a controlled contract phase

chronology_steps.goal: If rollback safety ended, prove the compensating release plan and preserve prior truth instead of rewriting history.
chronology_steps.reader_window_expectation: closed windows stay closed; historical reads rely on preserved frozen basis
chronology_steps.rollback_posture: FAILED_FORWARD or FAIL_FORWARD_ONLY with explicit owner and runbook lineage
chronology_steps.writer_posture: forward fix or compensating release owns correction

continuation_and_reclaim_rules.rule: Nightly duplicate suppression and batch allocation operate on a frozen envelope so migration or client-window drift does not silently reuse stale attempt identity.
Crash recovery and reclaim may transfer ownership but may not collapse recovery into a fresh run with different basis, schema, or client-window assumptions.
Branch selection and manifest claim identity keep request lineage, nightly window keys, and inheritance basis frozen across continuation or replay.
Nightly global stop conditions may halt batch progression before new control-plane work begins when release admissibility or authority safety is compromised.

historical_manifest_guards.rule: Sealed or in-progress manifests continue under their frozen schema bundle and config refs even when the default live bundle advances.
Historical replay uses preserved post-seal basis artifacts rather than recollecting live state.
Unreadable historical basis or schema-reader incompatibility fails closed instead of silently substituting a different reader.
Dropped columns, destructive rewrites, or meaning changes cannot begin while manifests that rely on the old shape may still replay or reconcile.
Release evidence carries the full reader-window and compatibility posture, not just the static writer bundle hash.
Restore and reopen safety are judged against the same protected reader-window and limitation posture as forward release promotion.

typed_gaps_or_deferred_decisions.summary: ADR-009 fixes migration chronology and evidence boundaries, but not the final migration-runner product or orchestration engine.
